overall_summary: ElizaOS is undergoing a significant architectural shift by decoupling the core agent server from application packages while simultaneously expanding native mobile capabilities for Android. Core developer Shaw addressed community concerns regarding market performance by confirming active work on Eliza Cloud and a token utility model via the x402 payment standard.
key_facts: The core agent server was decoupled from application-specific packages using a new plugin-lifecycle registration system on April 29.
Native local agent architecture for Android was implemented, featuring a foreground service for Bun runtime execution.
The $ELIZA token has been implemented as the default payment method in x402 for elizaOS and Milady infrastructure services.
Eliza Cloud infrastructure was migrated from Next.js to a Vite SPA with Hono Workers, utilizing R2 for image generation storage.
A major technical modernization was completed including upgrades to TypeScript, Node.js, and Bun across the repository monorepo.
Support for seven different blockchain networks was integrated into the plugin registry to enable multi-chain payments.
A new NoModelProviderConfiguredError was introduced to the framework to provide actionable feedback for agent misconfigurations.
open_questions: What is the specific nature of the 'round two' mentioned by Shaw?

new_issues_prs.title: V2.0.0 release
new_issues_prs.author: odilitime
new_issues_prs.significance: Consolidates CI and expands coverage to TypeScript, Python, and Rust while modernizing automation.

new_issues_prs.title: Enabled native agent execution on Android devices
new_issues_prs.author: lalalune
new_issues_prs.significance: Introduces a five-phase landing for local agent execution on mobile devices.

new_issues_prs.title: Decoupled agent server from specific apps
new_issues_prs.author: odilitime
new_issues_prs.significance: Improves framework modularity by removing app-specific hard imports from the core server.

overall_focus.claim: The project is currently focused on infrastructure modernization, specifically architectural decoupling and cross-platform portability for mobile and cloud environments.

discord_updates.summary: Shaw provided major updates on elizaOS development, confirming work on Eliza Cloud and autonomous agents with crypto features. He addressed token performance, noting that buybacks are contingent on profitability.
The community discussed the $ELIZA token integration into x402 infrastructure and the completion of Eliza v3 and Milady integrations.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Community members expressed concern over token price declines and potential exchange delistings, but maintained support for the dev team's building efforts.
Users requested increased communication frequency and the development of PR planning to balance technical focus with community needs.

strategic_insights.insight: Development work has transitioned to quieter, focused servers to avoid sentiment-driven distractions from token price volatility.
implications_or_questions: How will the team maintain transparency while operating in segmented servers?

strategic_insights.insight: By implementing $ELIZA as the default payment method in x402, the project is pivoting from speculative value to infrastructure-backed utility.
implications_or_questions: Is the current revenue-generating strategy sufficient to reach the profitability needed for buybacks?

market_analysis.observation: Solana's recent price decline was identified as a primary driver for concerns regarding elizaOS ecosystem token performance.
market_analysis.relevance: Direct correlation observed between L1 performance and project community sentiment.
